title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Audit and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strong authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA), and monitoring for unusual activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware from encrypting them.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and conduct regular security training for developers and IT staff to recognize and mitigate potential threats.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: id: pretty-print uses effective GID instead of effective UID for name lookup
description:
The id utility in uutils coreutils exhibits incorrect behavior in its "pretty print" output when the real UID and effective UID differ. The implementation incorrectly uses the effective GID instead of the effective UID when performing a name lookup for the effective user. This results in misleading diagnostic output that can cause automated scripts or system administrators to make incorrect decisions regarding file permissions or access control.

---
_Zellic finding 3.73. Reported in the Zellic

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The issue causes the id utility to provide misleading output regarding user identity, which could lead to incorrect decisions in file permission or access control management. However, the severity is low as it does not directly enable privilege escalation or compromise system integrity.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of uutils coreutils once available to address the issue.
Review and validate any automated scripts or tools that rely on the id utility for user identity verification to ensure they are not impacted by this behavior.
Implement additional logging or monitoring to detect potential misconfigurations caused by this issue.

title: kill: 'kill -1' parsed as PID -1, sending SIGTERM to all processes (system crash / DoS)
description:
`kill -1` is incorrectly parsed as a positional `pid = -1`; combined with the default SIGTERM this calls `kill(-1, SIGTERM)`, signaling nearly every process the caller can see. GNU `kill` recognizes `-1`/`-9` as signals and reports "not enough arguments".

```
$ kill -1        # uutils: kill(-1, SIGTERM) -> mass termination / crash
$ kill -1        # GNU: kill: not enough arguments
```

**Impact:** a user running `kill -1` mass-terminates processes, potentially crashing the system. Recommendatio

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35369.

title: install -D: symlink race in directory creation allows arbitrary file overwrite
description:
The `-D` path runs `fs::create_dir_all` on a pathname then later opens the destination via path-based `File::create`/`fs::copy`, neither anchored to a directory fd. Between the two, an attacker can replace a path component with a symlink, redirecting the write.

**Impact:** an attacker with concurrent write access to the destination tree can redirect a privileged `install -D` to an arbitrary location, enabling arbitrary file overwrite with attacker-controlled content. Recommendation: use dirfd-b

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35356.

title: cut: -s (only-delimited) ignored when delimiter is a newline
description:
The cut utility in uutils coreutils incorrectly handles the -s (only-delimited) option when a newline character is specified as the delimiter. The implementation fails to verify the only_delimited flag in the cut_fields_newline_char_delim function, causing the utility to print non-delimited lines that should have been suppressed. This can lead to unexpected data being passed to downstream scripts that rely on strict output filtering.

---
_Zellic finding 3.22. Reported in the Zellic *uutils core

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35343.

title: install: TOCTOU symlink race (unlink-then-create without O_EXCL) allows arbitrary file overwrite
description:
`copy_file` in `install/src/install.rs` removes the destination then recreates it by pathname via `File::create` / `fs::copy` without `O_EXCL`/`create_new`. Between the unlink and the recreate, a local attacker with write access to the destination directory can drop in a symlink and redirect the write.

**Impact:** when `install` runs privileged into an attacker-writable directory (staging/build paths), the race allows redirecting writes to arbitrary files and overwriting sensitive system files

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35355.

title: ln: rejects non-UTF-8 source filenames in target-directory mode
description:
In target-directory forms (`ln SOURCE... DIRECTORY`), `ln` rejects source paths with non-UTF-8 filename bytes, while GNU accepts them. Breaks GNU compatibility for byte-oriented filenames on Unix filesystems.

PoC:
```
name=$(printf 'bad_\377'); mkdir dst; : > "$name"; ln "$name" dst
# GNU: exit 0, creates dst/bad_\377 ; uutils: exit 1, dst empty
```

---
_Zellic private finding (zellic-ext/coreutils-private PR #84). Reported in the Zellic *uutils coreutils Program Security Assessment* (for Cano

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The issue affects compatibility between GNU and uutils coreutils when handling non-UTF-8 filenames in target-directory mode. This could lead to operational disruptions or errors in environments relying on byte-oriented filenames, but it does not pose a direct security threat such as data leakage or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Ensure compatibility testing between GNU and uutils coreutils in environments where non-UTF-8 filenames are used.
Update documentation to clarify the behavior of uutils coreutils and provide guidance for handling non-UTF-8 filenames.
Monitor upstream patches or updates to uutils coreutils for a potential fix to align behavior with GNU standards.

title: comm: lossy UTF-8 conversion silently corrupts non-UTF-8 output
description:
The comm utility in uutils coreutils silently corrupts data by performing lossy UTF-8 conversion on all output lines. The implementation uses String::from_utf8_lossy(), which replaces invalid UTF-8 byte sequences with the Unicode replacement character (U+FFFD). This behavior differs from GNU comm, which processes raw bytes and preserves the original input. This results in corrupted output when the utility is used to compare binary files or files using non-UTF-8 legacy encodings.

---
_Zellic fin

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The issue causes silent corruption of data when using the comm utility in uutils coreutils, particularly for non-UTF-8 encoded files or binary files. This could lead to incorrect comparisons and loss of data integrity, especially in workflows involving legacy encodings or binary data processing.
recommended_actions: Avoid using the affected version of uutils coreutils for processing non-UTF-8 or binary files.
Switch to GNU coreutils or another alternative that preserves raw byte input for file comparisons.
Monitor for updates or patches addressing this vulnerability in uutils coreutils.

title: mktemp: empty TMPDIR creates temp files in CWD instead of /tmp
description: The mktemp utility in uutils coreutils fails to properly handle an empty TMPDIR environment variable. Unlike GNU mktemp, which falls back to /tmp when TMPDIR is an empty string, the uutils implementation treats the empty string as a valid path. This causes temporary files to be created in the current working directory (CWD) instead of the intended secure temporary directory. If the CWD is more permissive or accessible to other users than /tmp, it may lead to unintended information disclosure or

title: The Reviews Widgets for Google, Yelp & TripAdvisor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored...
description: The Reviews Widgets for Google, Yelp & TripAdvisor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'page_id' shortcode attribute of the [fbrev] shortcode in versions up to and including 2.7.3. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in the Feed_Shortcode::fbrev() method, which passes the raw shortcode attribute through Feed_Old::get_feed() into the View::render() method, where it is echoed directly into the data-id HTML attribute without esc_a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute malicious scripts in the context of a user's browser by exploiting the stored XSS flaw. This could lead to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions on affected websites. The impact is significant for websites relying on this plugin for displaying reviews, especially if user trust is compromised.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Reviews Widgets for Google, Yelp & TripAdvisor plugin to a patched version if available. If no patch is available, disable the plugin temporarily.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.
Review and sanitize user inputs and outputs in custom WordPress plugins or themes to prevent similar vulnerabilities.

title: ColdFusion versions 2025.9, 2023.20 and earlier are affected by an Improper Input Validation...
description: ColdFusion versions 2025.9, 2023.20 and earlier are affected by an Improper Input Validation vulnerability that could result in arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user. Exploitation of this issue does not require user interaction. Scope is changed.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code without user interaction, potentially leading to system compromise, data theft, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by Adobe for affected ColdFusion versions.
Implement strict input validation and monitoring to detect and block suspicious activity.
Review and enhance access controls to limit the scope of potential exploitation.

referenced in the stream before the resulting object is cast to

title: BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access contain a high-severity pre...
description: BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access contain a high-severity pre-authentication vulnerability in the network communication subsystem. Insufficient validation of client-supplied input may allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to trigger a denial-of-service condition affecting appliance availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access allows unauthenticated remote attackers to exploit insufficient input validation, potentially causing a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. This could disrupt critical operations and availability of the affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches or updates provided by BeyondTrust to address CVE-2026-40140.
Implement network segmentation and restrict access to BeyondTrust appliances to trusted IP ranges to minimize exposure.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and configure intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS) to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A critical pre-authentication vulnerability exists in the authentication subsystem of BeyondTrust...

Improper validation of authentication data may allow a network-positioned attacker to bypass access controls and gain unauthorized access to the appliance, including accounts with elevated privileges. Exploitation requires a specific authentication configuration to be enabled

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a network-positioned attacker to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to BeyondTrust appliances, potentially compromising sensitive systems and data, including accounts with elevated privileges. This could lead to data breaches, system disruptions, and unauthorized control over critical infrastructure.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any patches or updates provided by BeyondTrust to address CVE-2026-40138.
Review and disable the specific authentication configuration that enables this vulnerability, if applicable.
Conduct a thorough audit of access logs to identify any signs of unauthorized access or exploitation.

title: A critical pre-authentication vulnerability exists in the authentication subsystem of BeyondTrust...

Exploitation requires a specific authentication configuration to be enabled.

title: chmod: --preserve-root bypassed by any path that resolves to root (e.g. /../)
description:
`Chmoder::chmod()` only compares the literal argument against `Path::new("/")`, so the `--preserve-root` guard is bypassed by any path that *resolves* to root — a symlink to `/` or simply `/../`.

```
if self.recursive && self.preserve_root && file == Path::new("/") {
    return Err(ChmodError::PreserveRoot("/".to_string()).into());
}
```

**PoC** — recursively chmods the entire filesystem to `000` despite `--preserve-root`:

```
chmod -R --preserve-root 000 /../ -v
```

**Impact:** `--preserve-

title: CVE-2026-13527 - A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System 1.0. The affected
description: A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /preview4.php. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform SQL injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database manipulation, and system compromise. Exploits are publicly available, increasing the risk of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'course_year_section' parameter in the /preview4.php file.
Update the SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System to the latest version if a patch is available.
Implement web application firewalls (WAF) to monitor and block malicious SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-13523 - A weakness has been identified in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/u
description: A weakness has been identified in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/utils/base_encoding.c of the component ISOBMFF Parser. Executing a manipulation can lead to highly compressed data. The attack needs to be launched locally. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This patch is called 297f2d8d1f493d8b241330533cd47f7da758aeb3. A patch should be applied to remediate this issue. The vendor confirms: "We added a check on inflate output size, if it surpasses 32 times the input size we stop in error. This value could be adjusted later."

title: CVE-2026-13514 - A weakness has been identified in Chess Play and Learn App up to 4.9.42 on Android. This issue affec
description: A weakness has been identified in Chess Play and Learn App up to 4.9.42 on Android. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file AndroidManifest.xml of the component com.chess. This manipulation causes exposure of backup file to an unauthorized control sphere. It is feasible to perform the attack on the physical device. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading the affected component is advised. The vendor was informed early about this issue. They confirmed the existence and that they will address it. Furthermore, they explain that their bug bounty "explicitly excludes physical-access attacks". However, they appreciate the quality of the report and aim at making a goodwill payment to the researcher.
